message.reasoning:
We need to produce JSON with kind, assistant_message, optionally tool_call, acquired_fact_ids, readiness_state. We are in a state where we need to gather evidence needed before editing schema definitions, staying versioned etc. The active code window is artifacts.rs, we have expanded file symbols already? The history shows expanding file symbols for artifacts.rs, tool_result session.expand_file@v1 status:ok, fields: [changed,operation,session_export,status,target]. So presumably we have evidence visible. Now we need to decide next step: maybe we need to hydrate source or examine schema version constants? The objective is to gather exact evidence needed before editing schema definitions so fixtures etc stay versioned. Likely we need to look at schema version constants definitions in artifacts.rs. Those are present at top: pub const FIXTURE_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION: u32 = 1; etc. So we need to retrieve those exact values. We can perhaps use graph description or find symbol definitions. Possibly use graph.find to locate symbol definitions? Or use graph.describe? The strategy says prefer graph discovery then focused lexical reads only when exact proof is blocking. We have evidence visible? readiness_state maybe evidence_visible? We haven't yet acquired exact proof of version numbers. So we may need to read the source to get the exact values. We can issue a tool_call to read the file? There is session.read? Not listed. There's session.expand_file which we already used. We could use session.hydrate? Or maybe use graph.describe to get details of symbols? Could use graph.find to locate symbols like FIXTURE_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION. But those are constants defined in the file. We could use graph.find with kind symbol? But we need to refer to short ids. The frontier recommends expand_file already done. Maybe next step is to hydrate source to get full context? The recommended action: hydrate_source [F1] relation=- direction=- candidates=0 priority=120 via session.hydrate(...). So perhaps we should issue a tool_call session.hydrate with target selector for the file? Or maybe we should use session.hydrate on the file to get source? The tool name is session.hydrate. Payload includes target selector. The target could be file path? Usually session.hydrate expects a selector like "path:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". So we could call session.hydrate with target "path:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". That would hydrate source, giving us full file content ready for reading. Then we could read the constants. However we might be able to directly read the symbols via graph.describe? graph.describe takes selector string; could describe the file? Might give metadata but not source. To get exact evidence (the values of constants), perhaps we need to read the file content. The session.hydrate would make the source available for reading. Then we could maybe use session.read? Not listed. There's no read tool. But we could use graph.find to locate symbols and then maybe session.expand_file again? Actually we already expanded file symbols; maybe that gave us some info but fields show [changed,operation,session_export,status,target] no actual content. So we need deeper hydration.

Thus we should issue a tool_call session.hydrate@v1 with target selector for the file. Then we will get hydrated source; after that we can extract the constants. But the response format expects tool_call with payload. So we can do that. Then acquired_fact_ids would be maybe list of fact ids we have acquired; we haven't got any yet? Might include something like "FIXTURE_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION". But we haven't retrieved it yet. Maybe we can include empty list for now. Or we could include the id of the constant? The short ids are like F1 etc referencing the file? Actually short ids are used for nodes; we have maybe a node id for the file? Not given. The graph frontier shows focus: crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs | visible_nodes=36 etc. The file node maybe has short id? Not provided. Could be "F1"? Actually earlier they used [F1] for file? In graph relations they show [F1] crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs --exports--> [S1]... So maybe F1 is the file node identifier. So we could refer to short id "F1". So acquired_fact_ids could include "F1"? But that's the file node, not the constant. Might be okay.

The readiness_state: we have evidence visible? We have expanded file symbols but not read content. We need to hydrate to get content. So readiness_state maybe "evidence_visible"? Or "not_ready"? The instructions: readiness_state can be "not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit". Since we have some evidence but not enough to edit, maybe "evidence_visible". Or we could set "evidence_acquired" after hydration? But hydration hasn't happened yet. So maybe "evidence_visible". Let's set readiness_state: "evidence_visible". Then we will issue a tool_call to hydrate.

Thus JSON:

{
  "kind":"tool_call",
  "assistant_message":"Hydrating source to extract schema version constants",
  "tool_call":{"tool_name":"session.hydrate","payload":{"target":"path:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs"}},
  "acquired_fact_ids":[],
  "readiness_state":"evidence_visible"
}

Make sure no extra prose. Provide JSON only.
